Bus Timings

Bus 11 Route and Stops in Dubai: Full Details

August 7, 2025
By Al Khaleej
Install
★★★★★ 5.0
Google Play Official App
UAE Bus Timing

Travel across the UAE with confidence. No confusion. No wasted time.

Bus 11 is an essential part of Dubai’s public transportation network, providing a vital connection between Rashidiya Bus Station Parking and Al Awir Immigration Office. With frequent stops, Bus 11 ensures convenient access to various local destinations, making it a practical choice for navigating the northern and eastern parts of Dubai.

Route Name 11
Route Description Rashidiya Bus Station Parking - Al Awir Immigration Office
Total Distance Approximately 22 kilometers
Total Journey Time Approximately 47 minutes
Operational Hours 05:00 to 22:00 (Depending on Starting Point)
Fare AED 3
Official Website RTA Dubai

Download PDF Bus 11

Bus Timings

Bus 11 operates frequently throughout the day, providing reliable service for both morning and evening commuters. Below are the bus timings:

  • From Al Awir Immigration Office: 05:00, 06:00, 07:05, 07:35, 08:05, 08:35, 09:05, 09:35, 10:05, 10:35, 11:05, 11:35, 12:05, 12:35, 13:05, 13:35, 14:05, 14:35, 15:05, 15:35, 16:05, 16:35, 17:05, 17:35, 18:05, 18:38, 19:10, 19:40, 20:10, 20:40, 21:10, 21:35, 22:00

Bus Ticket Price

The fare for Bus 11 adheres to Dubai’s standard public transport pricing. A single journey generally costs AED 3 with a Nol Card. Prices may vary based on the card type and the number of travel zones crossed. For the most precise fare information, passengers should check the RTA Dubai app or inquire at metro stations. Be sure to have your Nol Card ready for a smooth trip.

Bus Booking

Bus 11 does not offer online booking options. Passengers can board the bus directly at any of the designated stops along the route. To stay updated on real-time schedules and service information, the RTA Dubai app provides live tracking and detailed schedule information, helping passengers plan their journeys more effectively. The app also offers alerts for any service changes.

Bus Route

Bus 11 operates between Rashidiya Bus Station Parking and Al Awir Immigration Office.

  1. Rashidiya Bus Station Parking

  2. Rashidiya Bus Station · 3

  3. Mushrif, Eppco

  4. Mushrif, Masjid

  5. Mushrif, Park

  6. Mizhar 2, Turnoff

  7. ???? ????? ????????

  8. Khawaneej Road, 2

  9. Al Khawaneej Road 4

  10. Masjid Khawaneej

  11. Bin Bisher Farm

  12. Khawaneej, Boys School

  13. Rawabi Diary, Crossing

  14. Imam Al Shafi Masjid, Amardi

  15. Amardi, Road

  16. Amardi, Road 4

Recommended Guides

Enhance your daily commute with these essential transport guides:

Complete RTA Intercity Routes Guide Largest Bus Stations Infrastructure in UAE

  1. Etisalat

  2. CMC Roundabout

  3. Masjid

  4. Awir, Boys School

  5. Dubai Municipality Center

  6. Awir, Girls School

  7. Awir

  8. Emirates NBD

  9. Awir, Terminus

  10. Emirates Auction Yard

  11. Dubai Courts

  12. DEWA Office

  13. Al Awir Immigration Office

Bus 11 Station Overview

Rashidiya Bus Station Parking

Rashidiya Bus Station Parking is a central transit hub serving Bus Route 11, strategically located in the Rashidiya area. This station provides extensive parking facilities, making it convenient for commuters to switch between their vehicles and public transport. The station is well-equipped with amenities such as ticket counters, waiting areas, and real-time bus schedule displays. Its proximity to major residential and commercial zones enhances accessibility for both daily commuters and visitors.

Al Awir Immigration Office

The Al Awir Immigration Office station is a key stop for Bus Route 11, situated near the Al Awir Immigration Office. This station caters to individuals visiting the immigration office for various services and documentation. Its location provides easy access for those managing their immigration needs, offering a convenient link to the wider public transport network.

Tourist Destination Along the Way

Bus 11 travels through a mix of residential and commercial areas in Dubai, and while it primarily serves local neighborhoods and business districts, there are a few notable spots along the route:

  1. Mushrif Park: A large public park offering picnic areas, walking paths, and various recreational facilities, making it a popular spot for families and outdoor enthusiasts.

2.

  1. Dubai Courts: While not a tourist attraction per se, it is a significant landmark for those interested in the legal and administrative aspects of the city.

  2. Emirates NBD: As one of the leading banks in Dubai, it’s not a tourist attraction but a major financial institution in the city.

  3. Al Awir Immigration Office: A crucial administrative facility for those dealing with immigration matters, though not a tourist spot, it is a key location for residents and visitors.

For more leisurely exploration or cultural experiences, the stops around Mushrif Park and Al Khawaneej offer the most notable destinations.

Travelling with Careem

Using the Careem app, you can conveniently reach Al Awir Immigration Office, the final stop on Bus 11’s route starting from Rashidiya Bus Station Parking. The journey to this endpoint typically costs between AED 75 and AED 90. The app ensures a smooth ride with real-time fare estimates and route details, making it easy to plan your trip efficiently. This option offers a reliable way to travel to the farthest point on the Bus 11 route.

Bus 11 connects Rashidiya Bus Station Parking with Al Awir Immigration Office, covering a range of residential and commercial stops in Dubai. The route offers frequent service throughout the day, ensuring convenience for both daily commuters and travelers. With affordable fares and real-time updates available via the RTA Dubai app, Bus 11 is a practical choice for navigating the city. Its extensive coverage and reliable schedule make it an essential part of Dubai’s public transport network.

Official UAE Sources

For the latest schedules, fares, and policy updates, verify with these primary sources:

  • RTA Journey Planner
  • RTA Intercity Timetable Reference (E100)
  • Abu Dhabi Mobility
  • Sharjah Roads and Transport Authority (SRTA)

Editorial review date: August 7, 2025.

Written by Al Khaleej
